extraction.one_line_summary: KRISTOFF Alexander won Stage 1 in a surprise sprint finish ahead of PEDERSEN Mads, BENNETT Sam, and SAGAN Peter after wet, technical descents fragmented the peloton.
tactical_patterns: GREIPEL André lost 2 minutes solo on descent before regrouping, exposing veteran weakness in technical wet conditions early.
Multiple crashes within 3km banner nullified time gaps, rewarding late positioning over sustained pace-making through technical sections.
BENNETT Sam's crash mid-stage forced costly effort to rejoin on hard terrain, undermining sprint legs despite top-4 finish.
KRISTOFF Alexander wasn't originally selected for team Tour roster; late pandemic substitutions altered sprint hierarchy and expectations.

rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: positive: opportunistic winner despite low pre-race profile, first yellow at age 33 in eighth Tour
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: crashed early, regrouped but visibly fatigued in sprint after hard chase effort
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: second place from Trek leadout suggests genuine sprint speed, not just domestique role
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: fifth but consistent, clearly targeting green jersey accumulation over stage hunting
rider_signals.GREIPEL André: negative: dropped 2 minutes on wet descent, technical handling deterioration signals career decline
rider_signals.DE BUYST Jasper: negative: crashed on finish line circuit, compromising EWAN's leadout for finale
